chunks.text: Pejman Mar Ventures creates new competition for UC Berkeley entrepreneurs. Venture capital firm Pejman Mar Ventures has created a new competition for UC Berkeley entrepreneurs who demonstrate passion and dedication to making their vision a reality. They are offering $250,000 to back up a startup founded by a Berkeley student, faculty member or alum in exchange for 10 percent.
chunks.question: What does Pejman Mar Ventures offer in their competition?

chunks.text: Matei Zaharia and John Duchi win ACM dissertation awards. EECS alumni Matei Zaharia (advisor Profs. Scott Shenker & Ion Stoica) and John Duchi (advisor Prof. Michael Jordan) have won ACM (Assoc. for Computing Machinery) dissertation awards. Matei won the 2014 ACM Doctoral Dissertation Award for his thesis “An Architecture for Fast and General Data…
chunks.question: Which award did Matei Zaharia win?

chunks.text: Steve Wozniak and Yoky Matsuoka receive Distinguished Alumni Awards. Two EECS alumni have been chosen to receive distinguished alumni awards by the Cal Alumni Association (CAA). Steve Wozniak, (B.S. EE, ‘86) has been named 2015 Alumnus of the Year. He helped shape the computing industry with the design of Apple’s first line of products, co-founding Apple Computer Inc. with…
chunks.question: What award did Steve Wozniak receive?
